G. Carrino HO 31254 Ex 73 vd Exclusive for the Relations Officer and CORNING LEADER

September 15, Corning, N.Y. «New York State College of Education at Albany started classes on September 14, under the direction of Associate Dean Jack Derringer, ‘the: newly appointed Associate Dean for Academie Affairs at the College, according to an announcement by Dr. ivan R. Collins, President of the institution, Dr. Derringer had been Dean of the State University Corning Community College since its opening, three years ago. ‘The Albany College has been without a Dean since mid-July, when Dean Osear By Lanford was named president of the State University College of Rducation at Fredonia, Dr. Derringer will carry responsibility for several functions formerly @ischarged by the Dean of the College, especially in esademio counselling and the encouragement of scholarship.

The former Corning Dean was on hand to welcome terminal students of the Community College who are contiming their studies at Albany. They are: E. Keith Coombs, Louise Kammire, Barbara MeClure, and Robert Van Wagner. Three other residents of Corning, Samuel L. Cole, Andrea Lee Hanas, and Franklin E. Tobey, also are attending the College of Education at Albany,as freshmen.

Hee
